steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The function is $f(x)=ab^x+c$, so it is an exponential graph, not a constant horizontal line. The equation $f(x)=0$ asks for the $x$-values where the graph crosses the $x$-axis. On the shown graph, the curve stays entirely above or below the $x$-axis and never touches $y=0$.

steps.title: Use the graph shortcut
steps.content: Use the $x$-axis, which is the line $y=0$. Count intersections between the exponential curve and the $x$-axis. There are no intersections, so there are zero values of $x$ for which $f(x)=0$. That matches choice D, so D is correct.
